question: According to Table 1.3-1, how does NuScale's electrical output compare to a typical PWR?

nuscale_ch01_0189.title: BTP 6-2, Rev 3: Minimum Containment Pressure Model for PWR ECCS PerformanceAll (B.1 thru B.3)Various
nuscale_ch01_0189.summary: This section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria related to containment pressure and water chemistry for PWR ECCS performance. It concludes that certain criteria are not fully applicable to the NuScale design due to differences such as the absence of a containment spray system, sump, or secondary containment, and that some guidance (like BTP 6-2, Rev 3) is not applicable because a LOCA in NuScale does not result in core uncovery. Where relevant, NuScale applies the intent of the criteria to its unique design features.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, core design, and safety systems, showing that NuScale uses a smaller, simpler, and more integrated design with fewer components and lower power output per module compared to conventional PWRs. The comparison is summarized in tables for clarity.

nuscale_ch01_0006.title: Power Output
nuscale_ch01_0006.summary: The NuScale Power Plant consists of up to 12 NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), each rated at 160 MWt and producing approximately 50 MWe, for a total net output of about 570 MWe when accounting for house loads. The actual electrical output may vary depending on environmental conditions, and the design includes a 2% margin for measurement uncertainty.

nuscale_ch01_0439.title: SRP 10.4.6, Rev 3: Condensate Cleanup SystemII.2 Maintain indirect cycle PWR water quality to avoid corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary (GDC 14)
nuscale_ch01_0439.summary: The section outlines NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for maintaining indirect cycle PWR water quality to prevent corrosion-induced failure of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, as required by GDC 14. It states that the NuScale steam generator design differs from traditional designs, with primary water outside and secondary water inside the tubes, and no steam generator blowdown, resulting in different secondary chemistry requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0110.title: Figure 1.7-1b: Electrical Symbols
nuscale_ch01_0110.summary: This section provides a comprehensive legend of electrical symbols and abbreviations used in NuScale’s Final Safety Analysis Report drawings, including representations for switches, relays, transformers, and logic gates. It also explains the meanings of various indicator lights, control devices, and logic functions relevant to instrumentation and control systems.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features, as outlined in DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0. It details whether each regulatory guide is applicable, and if so, whether NuScale’s design fully or partially conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Several guides are noted as not applicable, while others are partially or fully met.

edges.description: Section A references regulatory criteria and partial conformance related to PWR systems, while Section B describes the NuScale containment and power output, connecting them through the comparison of NuScale's design and output to typical PWR standards as required by regulatory analysis.
